steps.title: Use the contrast
steps.content: The 'While' clause says recent scholarship has undermined claims about Ibn Rushd's works. The rest says his location made the works available far west of the center of Islamic thought, which would support a claim that his influence was limited.

steps.title: Choose the limited claim
steps.content: A introduces controversy, B introduces hostility, and C introduces imitation; none is raised by the text. The word must name the rejected limited-influence claim, not a new topic about tone, copying, or disagreement. The undermined claim must be that his works were not significant to contemporary Muslim philosophers. The answer is D.
